text: Historic Winterburn Farm is a spectacular 42 acre estate compound with sweeping views of the Hudson and Palisades beyond. Located in Westchester's bucolic hamlet of Pocantico Hills, the property offers the beauty and serenity of the country within 30 miles of Manhattan. The main residence, designed for Rodman Rockefeller by Architect Richard P. Donahue, is mid century modern. The home's central core is a great room with 20 foot ceilings and walls of glass facing the river. Equestrian facilities include a 14-stall stable, indoor and outdoor riding arenas, six fenced paddocks and two grooms's apartments. text: Step into the very year our nation was born and own a piece of American history! Originally constructed in 1776, this iconic stone residence later became the "Pawling Toll Gate and Toll House," serving as a vital gateway for travelers, a general store, a post office, and a legendary tavern. It is even whispered that George Washington himself once proved his strength within these walls, winning a bet by lifting a 400lb hogshead of beer. Inside, the home breathes character. Hand-hewn beams stretch across the ceilings, deep-set windows welcome the southern sun, and original fireplaces with wood stove inserts serve as the heart of the home. You'll walk on wide-plank "King's Wood" floors (massive boards harvested in colonial defiance of the British Crown) that have felt the footsteps of figures like Emilio "Dee" DaBramo. While the history is meticulously preserved, the convenience is modern, featuring a kitchen with custom wood cabinetry, granite countertops and sleek stainless-steel appliances. With 2 bedrooms and 2 full baths, the layout is both intimate and versatile. The lower level offers a private sanctuary perfect for an artist's studio, a quiet writer's retreat, or a distinctive guest suite. Outside, the 2.2 acre property is a nature lover's dream. Groomed trails wind through the land, passing dramatic natural stone features and leading directly to New York State land and the Appalachian Trail. Enjoy direct access to vast untouched nature while ensuring your backyard remains a private, wooded vista forever. Directly across the road, the Maybrook Trailway offers miles of adventure for cyclists and explorers. A two-story, two-car detached garage sits perched on the hill, offering even more potential for storage or creative conversion. Find yourself less than 10 minutes away from boutiques, restaurants, Pawling train station and the grocery store. This isnt just a house; it's a legacy in a great location. From storied stone walls to the premium modern finishes, every inch of this home has been meticulously maintained to honor it's 250-year legacy. From its revolutionary roots to its modern-day charm, this is a property without equal.

text: First Showing on Sunday March 8 at the Open House Perched on a high floor and bathed in natural light, this stunning one-bedroom plus home office corner residence offers loft-like living with soaring 12-foot ceilings. The gracious entry foyer welcomes you with exceptional storage, including a large closet designed to neatly organize coats, shoes, and everyday essentials. From there, a gallery-style hallway provides the perfect setting to display artwork and creates a dramatic arrival experience. The spacious living and dining area faces southwest, filling the home with warm afternoon light and sunset views. Beautiful bamboo flooring runs throughout the apartment. The open kitchen is thoughtfully appointed with limestone countertops, stainless steel appliances, rich wooden cabinetry, and glass tile backsplash. Finally, the home office easily adapts to your needs and can serve as a guest room, nursery, media room, or private workspace. BellTel Lofts was built in 1929 as the headquarter office of the New York Telephone Company and was considered Brooklyns first skyscraper. The building is a significant example of the art-deco style and was designed by Ralph Walker. Voted a landmark in 2004, the building was converted into apartments in 2008 by Beyer Blinder Belle. With 219 units spread across 27 stories, this highrise has a full-time doorman, as well as a garage, childrens playroom, lounge, storage units, fitness center, bike storage, and a rooftop terrace. BellTel is surrounded by eleven subway lines 2, 3, 4, 5, Q, B, A, C, F, G, and R. The CityPoint complex, just three blocks away, offers exceptional dining, shopping and entertainment options, such as the Dekalb Food Hall, Trader Joe's, Alamo Drafthouse Cinema, and One Medical location. Assessment: $160.30/month till December 2026

An adjoining den with stone details from the original Dutch stone farmstead leads to an expansive and bright sunroom where skylights and picture-perfect windows flood the space with daylight. A generously sized bedroom and full bath complete this wing making it ideal for multi-generational living, dedicated guest quarters, or even a home-based office suite with its own entrance and amenities. Down a striking set of stone stairs, the original stone cellar offers a unique space for a home gym, game room, or wine cellar. Completing the first floor is a half bathroom off the entry hall and an ample laundry room. The upper level, divided by the central staircase, includes three spacious bedrooms and a beautifully appointed bath with deep soaking tub and glass-enclosed shower in addition to the sweeping primary suite with cathedral ceilings, brick fireplace, and a loft overlooking the suite which is perfect for a reading nook, meditation space, or quiet work corner. The primary bath, clad in marble with a freestanding tub, offers a spa retreat. Every element of this home has been thoughtfully reimagined for modern comfort and long-term peace of mind. It has been completely overhauled including new kitchens and bathrooms, finishes and fixtures, plumbing and electrical, central air and heating system, and water filtration system. The exterior has also been upgraded with new siding, windows, shingle roof, and drainage system. Outside, are several distinct patio areas for lounging and dining as well as a custom fire pit for gathering under the night sky. Adjacent to the main house, a 7,000 sq ft rustic barn with five garage bays and a lofty upper level awaits your vision, whether a workshop, car collection, artist's studio, horse stables, or all of the above. Located in the Town of Marbletown, where Kingston meets Stone Ridge, Stone Manor is just minutes from popular towns with a host of dining, shopping and recreational options making it both a distinctive country escape and a vibrant base for exploring the cultural and natural riches of the Hudson Valley and Catskills. Make this singular estate your legacy!

Sunny's mother, Annie Laurie, later married Russell Barnett Aitken, a celebrated ceramic sculptor, sportsman, author, and big game hunter. Together, they pursued their shared interests in houses, English and French furniture, arms and armor, and fine decoration, as well as many philanthropic causes. The Annie Laurie Aitken English Galleries and The Russell Barnett Aitken Arms and Armor Galleries, both at the Metropolitan Museum of Art, represent their generosity to that institution located across the street from their home. After nearly 30 years of marriage, Annie Laurie died in 1984. Years later, Russell Aitken married Irene Boyd Roosevelt, the widow of John Aspinwall Roosevelt, the youngest son of Eleanor and President Franklin D. Roosevelt, who lived in the apartment until her passing in the spring of 2025. The proceeds from the sale of the apartment will be donated to the Frick Collection, the Metropolitan Museum of Art and the Morgan Library & Museum, continuing the tradition of cultural philanthropy embodied by its former residents. A legacy apartment like this very rarely comes on the market and is an opportunity not to be missed. text: Welcome to the Historic Town of Pine Plains! This completely renovated three bedroom home on a quiet country road will delight and intrigue you. The home dates back to the early 1900's and some of the original characteristics have been left in tact. This is most assuredly Old World Charm meets Modern Convenience. One bedroom on the first floor attached to the Sunroom, will work for a great Flex space, while the Primary bedroom upstairs with the large walk in closet is situated nicely near the additional upstairs bedroom. The hard wood floors, rustic ceiling beams and details of the handrail and doorknobs meld beautifully with the updated kitchen and bath finishes this builder has chosen. The Town of Pine Plains has been labeled by some as the Secret Gem of Dutchess County, dating back to the early 1700's. Stissing House, The Clock Tower, The Graham-Brush House, are just a few that will entice Historians. For outdoor enthusiasts you'll find a hike thru Thompson Pond Preserve, up Stissing Mountain and the Fire Tower or fishing and kayaking on Stissing Lake to be a fun day. The Stissing Center hosts various performances and events, and the Mashomack Polo Club allows spectators during their events. From Bootleggers to Opera singers, the area's history has evolved into what makes this quaint town in Northern Dutchess County a must see experience. Some photos virturally staged.

text: You have to come back and see this house with me because it's got that real "grandma's classic 1940s winter weekend" energy in the best way that has been on the short list feeling. Picture this: it's snowing outside, and we're posted up in the big living room with the granite fireplace going. Knotty pine paneling, bookshelves built in on both sides of the hearth, and that warm glow that makes you feel settled. We've got Monopoly spread out on the coffee table, a huge bowl of popcorn (the legit kind made on the stove), and a record playing... then playing again... then somehow playing again because that's what happens in houses like this. And of course there's always that one person in our group who breaks out a slide projector, vacation slides from back in the day with the vintage clothing, the hair styles, taking in the moments. This house just fits that vibe perfectly. Then you walk into the sunroom and it changes gears: slate floors under your feet, water views straight out toward the beach, and it becomes the reading and lounging zone. Newspapers, magazines, tuna melts, tomato and rice soup. It's basically the best relaxing room. Outside, it's the kind of winter that makes you want to actually do stuff: we can go sledding down Munn Lane, then heading to the Causeway beach to see what the sea rolled in, perfect for seaglass collecting. That whole shoreline exploration where you come back with cold cheeks and a pocket full of little treasures. The layout is great too, wood floors through most of the house, and there's that first-floor bedroom space (or den) with water views across the street that ties the whole country home feeling. It's cozy, practical, and somehow still feels special. And the garage... three bays, but it doesn't feel like "just a garage." It feels like a workshop where projects are always in motion. The kind of place where sleds, bikes, paddleboards, clam rakes, fishing poles, beach chairs, and coolers all have a home. Here's the kicker: it comes with two pieces of land, including the waterfront property across the street. So those water views? Protected. There's about 50 feet of sandy beach, and the path to the water is right across the street, perfect for kayaking or paddleboarding in the summer. And sitting on the hill, the elevation is high enough that you're not sitting stressing about flood issues. In the summer you're up on the hill watching fireworks in Orient Village, with views from the living room, sunroom, and that main-floor bedroom/den. It's peaceful, authentic, and it feels connected to the real local story-ice boat racing off Narrow River Road, oyster farming, clamming, fishing... all of it. This is what relaxation looks like when "country" means coastal, classic, and this is what we wanted.

text: Brookledge - A Timeless Country Manor. Every so often, a property comes along that makes you pause. Brookledge is that rare find-an extraordinary Country Manor built in 1923, rich with history, elegance, and craftsmanship nearly impossible to replicate today. Picture century-old trees echoing with memories: sunlit summer days, fireflies dancing in twilight, poolside laughter, joyful celebrations, and holidays that turn into lifelong stories. For decades, Brookledge has been the backdrop to moments that matter-and now, it's ready for its next chapter. Tucked at the edge of East Aurora's historic village, Brookledge offers privacy with ideal access. Just minutes from Route 400, you're conveniently connected to Buffalo, the airport, ski resorts, and Niagara Falls. East Aurora itself is more than a location-it's a lifestyle. This beloved village blends small-town charm with cultural richness, from boutiques and cafes to galleries, breweries, chocolate shops, and historic landmarks like Knox Farm and the Millard Fillmore House. A hub of the Arts & Crafts movement, it offers a vibrant blend of heritage and modern living. As you arrive at Brookledge, a 7,800-square-foot manor reveals itself across nearly nine acres of manicured grounds, towering evergreens, stone patios, and the serene whisper of Tannery Brook. Inside, the home features hand-carved black walnut woodwork, six fireplaces, stained and antique German glass, marble floors, arched doorways, and detailed craftsmanship throughout. The grand living room stuns with oak beams, a carved fireplace, and a marble floored sunroom with panoramic brook views. A refined dining room glows with leaded glass and a marble fireplace. The kitchen is both functional and elegant, with granite countertops, Sub-Zero appliances, a center island, pantry, and sunny breakfast nook. The primary suite is a true retreat with a double-sided fireplace, sitting room, spa-like bath, and Juliet balcony. A cozy library, family rooms, billiard room, kitchenette, and walkout lower level offer room to live, gather, and grow. A separate three-bedroom guest house, in-ground pool, and sport court complete this remarkable estate.

text: Set in the heart of Sag Harbor Village, [Redacted Entity] stands as both a work of architecture and a living chronicle of local history. Originally built in 1841 by Col. Henry Wentworth Hunt - publisher of The Sag Harbor Corrector, which later became The Sag Harbor Express - the house sheltered three generations of the Hunt-Johnson family before becoming a beloved gathering place for hosts Joe Petrocik and Myron Clement, whose circle famously included Truman Capote. Their imprint lives on in the estate's layered story, from the glass conservatory they salvaged at the Bridgehampton Horse Show to the echoes of evenings spent entertaining Sag Harbor's creative set. Fully reimagined, the home has been completely redone with an over three-year restoration led by Riostello Studio, MJS Building & Development, and Halcyon Design. The historic residence was carefully lifted to allow for a new foundation and expanded with a seamless 1, 400 +/- sq. ft. addition. Original elements - including solid timber porch columns, the front door with rim lock and sidelights, hand-hewn beams, and early bricks and granite from the chimneys and foundation - were meticulously preserved and repurposed throughout the property. Today, the 5, 000 +/- sq. ft. residence unfolds across three finished levels with five bedrooms and six bathrooms (plus 2 half baths). The interiors marry historic craftsmanship with modern sophistication: a gracious entry foyer opens to formal living and dining rooms, along with a Truman Capote-inspired lounge. The eat-in kitchen is anchored by custom white oak cabinetry, a leathered quartzite island, a La Cornue gas range, and top-tier appliances from Sub-Zero and Miele. Upstairs, three en suite bedrooms, including the primary suite, offer private retreats, while the lower-level guest/staff suite enjoys its own entrance. Wide-plank white oak, Belgian bluestone, custom millwork, and Waterworks fixtures lend refinement throughout, while amenities such as a fitness center with steam shower, radiant-heated baths, and Lutron home automation ensure comfort at every turn. Outdoors, the estate spans 0.38 +/- acres of newly landscaped grounds with an extensive investment in mature hornbeams, pleached lindens, and lush perimeter greenery. text: Set on nearly three acres in the heart of Warwick's historic village, Breckenridge is a commanding example of Greek Revival architecture-its stately brick facade, soaring columns, and symmetrical proportions echoing the grandeur of a bygone era. Built in 1911, the residence spans over 6,200 square feet across three levels, offering six bedrooms, five-and-a-half baths, and a wealth of original details including seven fireplaces, cathedral ceilings, and a formal dining hall and library. The home's architectural elegance is matched by its thoughtful layout, featuring a gourmet kitchen with gas range and pantry, impressive center hall with grand staircase, and elevator access. Add your own touches to this charming estate to bring back to full glory. This estate was built for Morris Rutherfurd, a prominent figure in Warwick's early 20th-century social and industrial landscape. Rutherfurd, served as president and general manager of the Lehigh & Hudson River Railroad and resided in Warwick until his death in 1944. His legacy reflects the influence of the Gilded Age-a period marked by opulence, industrial expansion, and architectural experimentation. The home's scale, craftsmanship, and stylistic choices are emblematic of that era's desire to blend classical European aesthetics with American ambition. Outside, the property continues to impress with a three-car garage, long private drive accommodating up to 20 vehicles, and a fenced, in-ground pool with pool house. Sprinkler systems in both front and rear gardens ensure year-round lushness, while the partly wooded grounds offer privacy and serenity just moments from Main Street. With its rich historical roots and timeless design, this mansion is not just a residence-it's a living chapter of Warwick's architectural and cultural heritage. Warwick, New York offers a rare blend of small-town charm, cultural richness, and natural beauty that appeals to both weekenders and full-time residents. Its historic village center is a walkable haven of 19th-century architecture, boutique shops, art galleries, and locally owned restaurants, creating a vibrant yet intimate atmosphere. The community thrives on connection, with seasonal festivals like Applefest, weekly farmers markets, and summer concerts that bring neighbors together. Nature lovers enjoy easy access to the Appalachian Trail, Mount Peter for skiing and hiking, and nearby Greenwood Lake and Wawayanda State Park for boating, fishing, and birdwatching. Recreational amenities include golf courses, parks, and playgrounds, while agritourism flourishes at local orchards, wineries, and creameries such as Pennings Farm and Bellvale Farms Creamery. Warwick also nurtures a strong creative spirit, with cultural institutions like the Warwick Historical Society and a drive-in theater that's been entertaining locals since 1950. Dining and shopping are elevated yet personal, with standout spots like Grappa Ristorante, Fetch Bar & Grill, and a variety of artisanal boutiques. Altogether, Warwick offers a lifestyle that's both refined and rooted-perfect for those seeking a deeper sense of place. Conveniently located 60 miles from New York City, less than [Redacted Entity]. Also available is direct bus service to Manhattan via NJ Transit. Ideal for any weekender or commuter.

All original details are intact and await to be lovingly restored in this nearly 6, 900/sf, 6-story home with a garden, roof terrace, and full basement (not included in the square footage). Abutting the property is the historic public garden Amster Yard (said to have been the terminal stop of the Boston Stage Coach on the Eastern Post Road in the 18th Century). Ideal for an end-user (who may want rental income), consulate, foundation, or a family office. Restore to the original setup below or reconfigure as needed. First Floor: Office with design room and private garden. Second and Third Floor: Duplex apartment consisting of living room, dining room, kitchen, 3 bedrooms, 2 baths, 2 fireplaces, and ample closet space. Fourth, Fifth, and Sixth Floors Triplex apartment consisting of living room, dining room, kitchen, 3 bedrooms, 3 baths, 2 fireplaces, ample closet space, and private roof terrace. Located on the historic Audrey Hepburn block, the homes were originally owned by artists, fashion designers, and actors. Currently, the consulates of Peru and Ukraine are on the block as well as the expansive Instituto Cervantes. Close to destination landmarks (including the United Nations and newly restored Waldorf Astoria) and all major transportation (including Grand Central Terminal).

This corner Tudor home was built in 1925, and is part of the sought-after community of Park Hill. The corner property is situated on .23 acres or 10, 018 square feet, along with a 2, 578 square foot home, and has two addresses [Redacted Entity], and [Redacted Entity]. There is an amazing long-stoned paver walkway from the street leading up to the entrance of the home (similar to strolling the yellow brick road). This corner Tudor home has seven large rooms, two and a half bathrooms, and a side entry oversized attached two-car garage with interior access. As you approach this home you are adorned by this arched gothic doorway. As you enter, you are immediately drawn to the interior rotunda. From the rotunda, you have a choice of walking to the lower level of the home through a solid eight panel wood door, or ascending a few wooden steeps, you reach an open hallway where you can choose your destination. Your choices would be the formal dining room, the enormous living roomwith a fireplace, a versatile office or bedroom, eat-in kitchen, or the washroom. As a corner property, the abundance of windows allows for considerable natural light to fill every room. An impressive, oversized wood staircase which would leads up to the sleeping quarters on the second floor. The platform on the staircase, can be set up as library along with a chair for a quiet reading area (as shown in the staged photo). Here you will find three generously proportioned bedrooms, and two full bathrooms. The oversized primary bedroom has an ensuite. The primary bedroom can easily accommodate a king-size bed, large furniture, and a sitting area. This primary suite is thoughtfully split from the other two bedrooms, providing an enhanced sense of privacy and tranquility. In the lower level, you can create an adult party area or a children's play zone in front of the fireplace. Adjacent to this versatile area is a pre-built bar, perfect for entertaining. Further, there is a separate laundry room, a laundry chute, and a separate entry into your oversized two-car garage. The deck off the kitchen is a perfect central hub for warm-weather entertaining, ideal for hosting barbecues or relaxing with sunset drinks and canaps. The expansive surrounding land offers endless possibilities for creating distinct seating and play areas for all your guests. This home offers a great commuter location, just a five-minute drive from the train station, with a quick 35-minute Metro-North ride putting you right in the heart of Grand Central, Manhattan. History: The Park Hill community name is derived from Robert Parkhill Getty, a New York executive who owned a 25-acre estate on South Broadway. In 1888, Getty sold the land to Andrew S. Brownell, a founder of the American Real Estate Company, which sought to create an A-lister getaway. Trains would carry visitors to a train station on what is now known as Undercliff Street. Brownell developed this community leaving many natural features. During development of this single-family community, Brownell left areas for parks and green spaces. The Racquet Club, a private neighborhood institution serving the Park Hill community, is a five-minute walk from this Tudor home. Originally established as the Park Hill Country Club in 1892, the club was rebuilt in 1965 after the original clubhouse was destroyed by fire. Today, members can enjoy amenities that include tennis courts, a pool, a bowling alley, and a lounge for adults. The venue is also available to be rented for private events. text: Welcome to a townhouse-like two-bedroom, two-bathroom residence of exceptional scale and character, featuring a private atrium terrace within The Level Club Condominium, an architecturally distinctive, full-service building on one of the Upper West Side 's most coveted blocks just moments from Central Park. Rather than a single flat plane, the residence unfolds vertically, with layered volumes, coffered ceilings, and subtle elevation shifts that define each space while preserving a seamless, loft-like flow. Expansive living and dining areas offer exceptional width and scale, creating an effortless setting for both intimate living and grand entertaining. The open chef 's kitchen anchors the home, appointed with custom wood cabinetry, stone countertops, premium stainless steel appliances, and generous island seating that connects naturally to the surrounding living space. A thoughtful split-bedroom layout ensures privacy and separation, with an oversized primary suite offering generous proportions, ample storage, and space for a sitting area or work-from-home retreat. Two well-appointed bathrooms, including a spacious primary bath, serve both everyday living and guests with ease. Warm hardwood floors, discreet custom storage, and refined detailing throughout reinforce a sense of quiet sophistication, creating a home that feels private, composed, and remarkably insulated from the city beyond. Originally constructed between 1925 and 1927 as an exclusive private members ' club and hotel by a Masonic society known as the Levellers, The Level Club is distinguished by its symbolism, craftsmanship, and architectural intention. Inspired by the Biblical description of King Solomon 's Temple, the building 's facade and dramatic proportions reflect a level of design and ambition rarely replicated today. Thoughtfully converted to residential condominiums in 1984, The Level Club retains its historic exterior and iconic lobby, offering distinctive Upper West Side residences shaped by the grandeur and scale of its original club-hotel layout. A true condominium offering flexibility for a primary residence, pied--terre, or international ownership.

text: A beautifully maintained 3-bedroom, 2-bath ranch offering comfort, character, and recent updates throughout -- a true forever home designed for both everyday living and special moments. The stunning raised wood ceiling in the living room, fresh paint, new flooring and large floor to ceiling windows adds a bright and luxurious charm. Enjoy cozy evenings by the pellet stove in one living space, while a wood fireplace creates the perfect ambiance in the dining/family room--ideal for gatherings and holiday celebrations. The home sits on a desirable double-sized lot spanning two parcels, providing exceptional outdoor space for entertaining, gardening, or simply relaxing. The attached, insulated garage adds year-round convenience and efficiency. Downstairs, the spacious basement offers incredible versatility with wide interior stairs for easy access. You'll find a separate utility room, dedicated laundry room, a full bathroom, and a generous bonus room that could easily serve as a workout room or be transformed into a fourth bedroom, home office, or craft room. The expansive layout provides plenty of storage and additional living potential.. Located just 15 minutes from Exit 30 of the NYS Thruway and 15 minutes to local grocery stores, this home offers the perfect balance of peaceful village living with convenient access to amenities. Situated in Saint Johnsville--a quiet, historic village along the Erie Canal--you'll enjoy a public boat launch, camping area, and a wonderful large public park that makes this a truly family-oriented community. Set in a pleasant residential neighborhood, this home offers both tranquility and a welcoming sense of community. If you're looking for space, warmth, move-in ready, and a place to truly put down roots, this inviting home is ready to welcome you.
